[BUG] Routes Sonnet created its own PLATFORM_PRESETS with different keys than worker's CODEC_PRESETS — had to add _PRESET_MAP bridge manually. Also Sonnet named methods differently (cancel_job vs canc

Routes should import CODEC_PRESETS directly from the worker instead of duplicating — single source of truth for encode params. Also, SSE (Server-Sent Events) for progress would be better than polling.

Routes Sonnet created its own PLATFORM_PRESETS with different keys than worker's CODEC_PRESETS — had to add _PRESET_MAP bridge manually. Also Sonnet named methods differently (cancel_job vs cancel, get_job_status vs get_status) — needed cross-file API alignment.

[BUG] No showFrameViewerSplit in store — had to use local state + CustomEvent. Alpha should add this to useCutEditorStore for persistence across panel switches. Also the two video elements load the sa
Could add horizontal split mode (top/bottom) in addition to vertical. Also a wipe mode where the split follows the mouse cursor rather than a fixed line — more intuitive for colorists.

No showFrameViewerSplit in store — had to use local state + CustomEvent. Alpha should add this to useCutEditorStore for persistence across panel switches. Also the two video elements load the same source twice — doubles memory for large files.

[BUG] EffectsPanel toggles (hflip/vflip) had no store wiring at all — handleToggle only wrote to local React state, so flips were lost on re-render. Fixed by adding storeKey to ToggleDef + wiring thro
Backend EFFECT_DEFS → FFmpeg filter mapping should mirror these exact ClipEffects keys. A shared type (or JSON schema) between frontend and backend would prevent drift.

EffectsPanel toggles (hflip/vflip) had no store wiring at all — handleToggle only wrote to local React state, so flips were lost on re-render. Fixed by adding storeKey to ToggleDef + wiring through setClipEffects.

[WORKED] asyncio.create_subprocess_exec in routes is better for FastAPI than the sync Popen in the service — no thread pool needed, native async streaming of stdout lines. SSE via StreamingResponse wi

Routes Sonnet built its own async YouTubeFetchManager inline instead of importing youtube_service.py. Both work but it's duplication — the sync service is useful for CLI/test contexts, async manager for FastAPI. Should consolidate to one implementation.

[WORKED] Desaturated channel colors (#aa6666 etc.) still clearly distinguish R/G/B while matching the muted NLE aesthetic. The color correction exception to ZERO color needs a gradient, not a binary —
Could add a color-blind mode that uses dashed/dotted line styles instead of color to distinguish R/G/B channels. Accessibility win for deuteranopia editors.

CurveEditor.tsx existed only in main, not in cut-ux worktree. Had to git checkout from main. This means worktrees can drift — new files added to main don't appear in agent worktrees until explicit checkout or rebase.
[BUG] CurveEditor wasn't in worktree when original monochrome task was done — file drift between main and worktree caused the miss.
[WORKED] Separate task tb_1774497973_1 already fixed it before Delta's needs_fix arrived.
Monochrome lint should scan ALL .tsx files in main, not just ones listed in a task's allowed_paths.
CurveEditor wasn't in worktree when original monochrome task was done — file drift between main and worktree caused the miss.

[BUG] Sonnet agents consistently create method names that don't match across files — submit_job(params) vs submit_job(name, params), budget vs _budget, list_active_jobs vs get_active_jobs, update_limi
A contract spec (like an OpenAPI schema or a Python Protocol class) shared across all Sonnet agents would eliminate the method name drift. Could auto-generate from the base_provider ABC.

Sonnet agents consistently create method names that don't match across files — submit_job(params) vs submit_job(name, params), budget vs _budget, list_active_jobs vs get_active_jobs, update_limits vs set_limits. Cross-file API alignment is the #1 integration tax with parallel agents.

[BUG] Two parallel save hooks exist: useCutAutosave (in CutEditorLayoutV2) and useCutSaveSystem (in CutStandalone). Both try to save. Autosave timer logic is duplicated. Should consolidate into one ho

Two parallel save hooks exist: useCutAutosave (in CutEditorLayoutV2) and useCutSaveSystem (in CutStandalone). Both try to save. Autosave timer logic is duplicated. Should consolidate into one hook — useCutSaveSystem is the better one now with recovery+beforeunload, useCutAutosave can be removed.
[WORKED] Gap analysis via parallel Sonnet agents (recon + audit) completed in ~6 minutes covering 50+ pytest files and 43 E2E specs. Two-tier approach (store-level + fixme panel tests) keeps CI green

Save endpoint moved to cut_routes_render.py but MARKER_B41 comment in cut_routes.py still references CutSaveRequest/cut_save_project — stale comment. Also, SaveIndicator.tsx, AutoMontagePanel.tsx, TimelineInstancePanel tab rows all lack data-testid attrs — Gamma should add them.
[BUG] Dual save hooks (useCutSaveSystem + useCutAutosave) both subscribe to store — potential conflict if both modify hasUnsavedChanges simultaneously
[WORKED] Removing dead Zustand selectors is a clean fix — no behavioral change, just eliminates unnecessary re-render cycles
Consolidate useCutSaveSystem and useCutAutosave into single hook — they split save/autosave/recovery across two hooks unnecessarily
Dual save hooks (useCutSaveSystem + useCutAutosave) both subscribe to store — potential conflict if both modify hasUnsavedChanges simultaneously
[BUG] Task description was stale — publishDialog collision and linkUnlinkClips missing handler were already fixed. Only the 4 missing actions remained.
Task description was stale — publishDialog collision and linkUnlinkClips missing handler were already fixed. Only the 4 missing actions remained.

[BUG] Brace-balance check on TSX files gives false positives — JSX conditional rendering creates minor imbalances. Fixed with ±5 tolerance. Also found 4 duplicate data-testid values across CUT compone

[WORKED] Parallel Sonnet research agents (3x) covered 44 E2E specs + 76 components + monochrome enforcement in ~90 seconds. Then 2 Sonnet writers produced both files in parallel. Total 5 Sonnets, zero

The debrief audit E2E spec could be extended with a DA5: seek boundary test — verify playhead stays within 0..duration after rapid keyboard navigation. This was mentioned in the original Epsilon-2 exp

Brace-balance check on TSX files gives false positives — JSX conditional rendering creates minor imbalances. Fixed with ±5 tolerance. Also found 4 duplicate data-testid values across CUT components (cut-timeline-ruler cross-file, media-info-panel/marker-list-panel/track-resize-handle same-file condi
[WORKED] Vite build passes cleanly after 56-commit merge — no TS errors. 4873/4904 python tests pass. Core backend (API, FFmpeg, DAG, task board) is rock solid.
Monochrome static analysis test should run as pre-merge gate, not just post-merge. Also: a per-file import chain validator could catch BUG-1 type regressions before merge.

BUG-1 CRITICAL: run_favorites_assembly stubs deleted from pulse_auto_montage.py breaking 38 tests (import chain). BUG-2: transitions panel removed from presetBuilders.ts but test expected 12 panels. BUG-3: _extract_predecessor_advice_from_json removed from generate_claude_md.py.
[BUG] ProRes/DNxHR not supported by WebCodecs — most color-grading workflows won't benefit in Phase 1. fetchKeyframeChunk uses approximate byte-offset range requests, not proper MP4 demuxer.
[WORKED] 25x latency reduction (126ms→5ms) for H.264/VP9/AV1. CSS filter mapping for exposure/contrast/saturation/hue covers most common color adjustments without server roundtrip.
ProRes/DNxHR not supported by WebCodecs — most color-grading workflows won't benefit in Phase 1. fetchKeyframeChunk uses approximate byte-offset range requests, not proper MP4 demuxer.
[WORKED] Pure stdlib implementation (no opentimelineio dependency needed) — json + xml.etree covers all 4 formats. Format auto-detection via extension + content sniffing is robust.
EDL adapter produces empty source_path — needs media relinking UI. FCPXML rational time parsing may have edge cases with unusual frame rates (29.97 drop-frame).
Web Audio API AnalyserNode for real-time VU/PPM meters in AudioMixer — no server roundtrip needed.
Audio scrubbing state is in-memory only (Python side) — resets on server restart. Pre-existing build error: EFFECT_APPLY_MAP not exported from EffectsPanel (TimelineTrackView.tsx).
[BUG] TestStructure had 8 more stale assertions beyond the import error — all referenced removed sections.
[WORKED] SLIM template produces clean bootstrap CLAUDE.md. All dynamic context via session_init is the right pattern.
Auto-generate test structure assertions from template AST — detect section drift before it becomes stale tests.

[WORKED] 6 parallel Sonnet research agents covering different files returned in ~100s each with precise line numbers and full codepath analysis. Zero overlap, zero wasted context. The UNINDEXED column

The debrief_skipped detection revealed 10 real tasks that lost their Q3 ideas. Next step: when Commander runs action=debrief_skipped, offer to re-open a mini-debrief session with the original agent (r
TestStructure had 8 more stale assertions beyond the import error — all referenced removed sections.

Extract markClip/matchFrame into store methods so any component can call them without duplicating the lane-scanning logic
